Title: Place a CT chest with contrast radiology order from an active visit
Preconditions: Clinician with Order Radiology privilege; patient has an active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard with active visit | 2. Open Orders entry | 3. Select radiology orderable CT Chest with contrast | 4. Set priority Routine, set clinical indication | 5. Sign and save the order
Test_Data: Orderable=CT Chest with contrast; Priority=Routine; Indication=Suspected pulmonary embolism
Expected_Result: Order is created in NEW/ACTIVE state, appears in active orders with orderer, datetime and indication; an order event is auditable

Title: Reject radiology order without a clinical indication
Preconditions: Clinician with Order Radiology privilege; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Begin a new radiology order | 2. Select orderable XR Wrist | 3. Leave clinical indication blank | 4. Attempt to sign and save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation message that clinical indication is required; no order is persisted

Title: Reject radiology order placement without Order Radiology privilege
Preconditions: User authenticated without radiology ordering privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as user lacking Order Radiology privilege | 2. Navigate to Orders entry | 3. Attempt to add a radiology orderable
Expected_Result: Radiology ordering action is not available or is blocked with authorization error; no order is created; denial is auditable

Title: Laterality required for paired-anatomy imaging orderable
Preconditions: Clinician with ordering privilege; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Start radiology order | 2. Select XR Knee (paired anatomy) | 3. Do not specify laterality | 4. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save blocked requiring laterality (Left/Right/Bilateral); order not persisted until laterality provided

Title: STAT priority order surfaces at top of worklist with STAT flag
Preconditions: Modality worklist accessible; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Place radiology order with priority STAT | 2. Open modality worklist | 3. Observe ordering and flags
Expected_Result: Order appears flagged STAT and is sorted ahead of Routine orders in the worklist

Title: Block contrast order for patient with documented contrast/iodine allergy
Preconditions: Patient has active allergy to iodinated contrast; clinician ordering
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with iodinated contrast allergy | 2. Order CT Abdomen WITH contrast | 3. Observe safety interaction at sign time
Test_Data: Allergy=Iodinated contrast (severe); Orderable=CT Abdomen with contrast
Expected_Result: A hard contrast-allergy alert fires; clinician must acknowledge with override reason or change to non-contrast; event is auditable

Title: Contrast allergy override requires documented reason
Preconditions: Patient with contrast allergy; allergy alert configured as override-with-reason
Test_Steps: 1. Order contrast study for allergic patient | 2. Trigger alert | 3. Attempt override without reason | 4. Provide reason and override
Test_Data: Allergy=Iodine; OverrideReason cases: blank, "premedicated per protocol"
Expected_Result: Blank reason is rejected; valid reason permits override and is stored with overriding provider and timestamp

Title: Renal function (eGFR) decision table for contrast administration
Preconditions: Latest eGFR observation available for patient
Test_Steps: 1. For each eGFR band order contrast study | 2. Observe gating behavior
Test_Data: eGFR cases: >=60 (clear); 30-59 (caution warn); <30 (hard block/nephrology); none (warn missing)
Expected_Result: eGFR>=60 allows; 30-59 warns and requires acknowledgment; <30 blocks or requires senior override; missing eGFR prompts to obtain value

Title: Pregnancy status warning for ionizing-radiation imaging
Preconditions: Female patient of childbearing age; pregnancy status recorded or unknown
Test_Steps: 1. Order CT/XR (ionizing) for patient | 2. Observe pregnancy safety prompt | 3. Acknowledge or document LMP/pregnancy status
Expected_Result: A pregnancy/radiation safety warning is displayed; clinician must confirm status or justify before proceeding; acknowledgment auditable

Title: Metformin hold advisory for iodinated contrast in at-risk patient
Preconditions: Patient on metformin with renal risk; ordering contrast CT
Test_Steps: 1. Order iodinated-contrast study | 2. Observe metformin advisory | 3. Acknowledge advisory
Test_Data: Med=Metformin active; eGFR=40; Orderable=CT Abdomen with contrast
Expected_Result: Advisory to hold metformin around contrast is shown and acknowledged; advisory acknowledgment recorded

Title: Schedule radiology order into an available modality slot
Preconditions: Approved radiology order exists; modality schedule configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open scheduling for the pending order | 2. Select modality and available slot | 3. Confirm appointment | 4. Verify slot booked
Expected_Result: Order is scheduled to the selected slot; slot shows as booked; patient/order linked to appointment

Title: Prevent double-booking of the same modality slot
Preconditions: One modality slot already booked
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to schedule a second order into an already-booked slot | 2. Confirm
Expected_Result: System rejects the conflicting booking or warns of overlap; original booking remains intact

Title: Boundary: schedule at the exact slot start and end boundaries
Preconditions: Modality schedule with defined slot boundaries
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule an order at the first available minute of a slot | 2. Schedule another at the last valid minute | 3. Attempt one minute past end
Expected_Result: Bookings at start and last valid minute succeed; booking at/after the end boundary is rejected

Title: Pairwise: modality x priority x patient-type slot routing
Preconditions: Multiple modalities and priorities configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create orders across modality/priority/patient-type combinations | 2. Observe routing/slot eligibility
Expected_Result: Each combination routes to an eligible slot per configuration; STAT bypasses routine queue; no invalid combination is bookable

Title: DICOM Modality Worklist exposes scheduled procedure step for booked order
Preconditions: DICOM MWL/HL7 integration configured; order scheduled
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule a radiology order | 2. Query Modality Worklist from modality/SCU | 3. Inspect returned worklist item
Test_Data: Order=CT Head; Accession auto-generated; PatientID synthetic
Expected_Result: MWL returns a Scheduled Procedure Step with correct patient name/ID, accession number, modality and scheduled datetime

Title: Accession number is unique and immutable per scheduled procedure
Preconditions: Order scheduling generates accession numbers
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule two distinct orders | 2. Inspect persisted accession numbers | 3. Attempt to reuse an accession on a new order
Test_Data: Order A, Order B; Accession format ACC-YYYY-NNNNNN
Expected_Result: Each order receives a unique accession; duplicates are rejected at the database/constraint level; accession does not change after assignment

Title: Worklist filters by modality, date and status
Preconditions: Worklist populated with mixed orders
Test_Steps: 1. Open worklist | 2. Filter by modality=CT | 3. Add date=today | 4. Add status=Scheduled
Expected_Result: Only orders matching all active filters are listed; clearing filters restores the full list

Title: State transition: Ordered to Scheduled to In-Progress to Completed
Preconditions: Order placed; scheduling and acquisition workflow available
Test_Steps: 1. Place order (Ordered) | 2. Schedule (Scheduled) | 3. Begin acquisition (In-Progress) | 4. Complete acquisition (Completed)
Test_Data: Order=MRI Brain; sequence of valid transitions
Expected_Result: Each forward transition is permitted and timestamped; status history reflects full ordered path with actor per step

Title: State transition: invalid backward transition from Completed to Scheduled is rejected
Preconditions: Order in Completed state
Test_Steps: 1. Open a Completed order | 2. Attempt to set status back to Scheduled
Expected_Result: Illegal transition is rejected with an error; status remains Completed; attempt is auditable

Title: State transition: order may be Cancelled from Ordered or Scheduled but not after Completed
Preconditions: Orders in various states
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel an Ordered order | 2. Cancel a Scheduled order | 3. Attempt to cancel a Completed order
Expected_Result: Cancellation succeeds from Ordered and Scheduled; cancellation of Completed is rejected; reason captured for valid cancellations

Title: Cancel a radiology order with mandatory reason and discontinue date
Preconditions: Active radiology order; clinician with discontinue privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open active order | 2. Choose Discontinue/Cancel | 3. Enter reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Order moves to Cancelled/Discontinued state with reason, actor and timestamp; no longer appears in active acquisition queue

Title: Reject cancellation without a reason
Preconditions: Active radiology order
Test_Steps: 1. Open active order | 2. Choose Cancel | 3. Leave reason empty | 4. Attempt save
Expected_Result: Cancellation is blocked requiring a reason; order remains active

Title: Create a preliminary (wet read) report on a completed study
Preconditions: Study Completed; radiologist with reporting privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open completed study | 2. Start a report | 3. Enter preliminary findings | 4. Save as Preliminary
Expected_Result: Report saved in Preliminary status, flagged as not final, attributed to the radiologist with timestamp

Title: Dictation draft autosave and resume without data loss
Preconditions: Radiologist drafting a report
Test_Steps: 1. Begin dictating/typing a report | 2. Navigate away or session interruption | 3. Return to the study report
Expected_Result: Draft content is preserved and recoverable; no loss of dictated text on resume

Title: Sign and finalize a report transitioning Preliminary to Final
Preconditions: Preliminary report exists; radiologist credentialed to sign
Test_Steps: 1. Open preliminary report | 2. Review findings/impression | 3. Sign/Finalize
Expected_Result: Report transitions to Final, locked from free edits, attributed to signing radiologist with signature datetime; finalization auditable

Title: Prevent editing a finalized report except via addendum
Preconditions: Final report exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open a Final report | 2. Attempt to modify the original body directly
Test_Data: Report status=Final; Edit attempt on body
Expected_Result: Direct edit of finalized content is blocked; only addendum path is offered; original final text remains immutable

Title: Add an addendum to a finalized report
Preconditions: Final report exists; radiologist with addendum privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open Final report | 2. Choose Add Addendum | 3. Enter addendum text | 4. Sign addendum
Test_Data: Addendum=Correction: laterality is right not left
Expected_Result: Addendum is appended with its own author and timestamp; original report preserved; both visible in report history

Title: Reject empty addendum submission
Preconditions: Final report exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Final report | 2. Choose Add Addendum | 3. Leave addendum text blank | 4. Attempt sign
Expected_Result: Empty addendum is rejected; no addendum is recorded

Title: Critical/panic finding requires documented communication and acknowledgment
Preconditions: Study with a critical finding; critical-result workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Finalize report flagged Critical | 2. Record communication to ordering provider | 3. Capture acknowledgment
Expected_Result: Critical finding requires documented notification (who/when/how) and provider acknowledgment before workflow closes; all logged

Title: Report turnaround time computed from completed acquisition to final sign
Preconditions: Study with completion and final-sign timestamps
Test_Steps: 1. Complete acquisition | 2. Finalize report later | 3. View turnaround metric
Expected_Result: Turnaround time is computed correctly (45 min) and reflected in reporting/metrics

Title: Open study images in PACS viewer from the order/report
Preconditions: PACS integration configured; study has images by accession
Test_Steps: 1. Open completed study | 2. Click View Images / PACS link | 3. Verify viewer launches for correct accession
Expected_Result: PACS viewer opens the correct study matched by accession/patient; images correspond to the order

Title: Handle PACS link when no images are available yet
Preconditions: Order scheduled/in-progress, images not yet acquired
Test_Steps: 1. Open in-progress study | 2. Attempt to open PACS images
Expected_Result: A clear no-images-available message is shown; no broken viewer or error stack is exposed

Title: Image-order patient mismatch is detected and blocked
Preconditions: Test environment to simulate accession/patient mismatch
Test_Steps: 1. Configure a study whose PACS images map to a different patient ID | 2. Attempt to open images from the order
Expected_Result: Mismatch is detected; viewer does not display another patient's images; safety/security error is raised and logged

Title: Inbound ORM^O01 radiology order message creates an order
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface configured for orders
Test_Steps: 1. Send a valid ORM^O01 order message | 2. Inspect created order
Test_Data: Message=ORM^O01; OBR for CT Head; synthetic PID
Expected_Result: An order is created matching message content (patient, procedure, priority); positive ACK returned

Title: Inbound ORU^R01 result message attaches report to the correct order by accession
Preconditions: HL7 results interface configured; order with accession exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 carrying report text referencing the accession | 2. Inspect order/report
Test_Data: Message=ORU^R01; OBR accession=ACC-2026-000123; OBX impression text
Expected_Result: Report content attaches to the matching order by accession; observation values mapped correctly; ACK returned

Title: Reject malformed HL7 message with negative acknowledgment
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message with a missing required segment/field | 2. Inspect ACK and persistence
Expected_Result: Message is rejected with AE/AR negative ACK; no partial order persisted; error logged

Title: Order status change emits HL7 status update (ORM/ORU status)
Preconditions: Outbound HL7 status interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Transition order to In-Progress then Completed | 2. Capture outbound HL7 messages
Test_Data: Transitions emit status fields (SC, IP, CM)
Expected_Result: Outbound messages reflect correct order status codes per transition with consistent accession/patient identifiers

Title: Create radiology order as FHIR ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; auth token with write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a ServiceRequest with imaging code | 2. Validate response and persisted resource
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest is created with status active, intent order, subject, code and authoredOn; 201 with Location header returned

Title: Retrieve imaging report as FHIR DiagnosticReport with category Radiology
Preconditions: Finalized imaging report exists; FHIR read scope
Test_Steps: 1. GET DiagnosticReport for the study | 2. Validate fields
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport returns category Radiology, status final, links to subject and ServiceRequest, and includes conclusion/result

Title: ImagingStudy resource exposes modality, series and accession
Preconditions: Completed study with DICOM metadata; FHIR ImagingStudy supported
Test_Steps: 1. GET ImagingStudy for the accession | 2. Validate modality/series/identifier
Expected_Result: ImagingStudy returns correct modality, series/instance counts and accession identifier mapped to the order

Title: Reject FHIR ServiceRequest with invalid status code
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST ServiceRequest with status=banana | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns 400/422 with OperationOutcome indicating invalid status; resource not created

Title: FHIR read of another patient's imaging report denied by scope
Preconditions: SMART scopes enforced; token limited to one patient context
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate with patient-scoped token for Patient A | 2. GET DiagnosticReport belonging to Patient B
Expected_Result: Request is denied (403/empty bundle); Patient B PHI is not disclosed; denial auditable

Title: API: place order missing required body fields returns structured error
Preconditions: REST API available with auth
Test_Steps: 1. POST order create with missing patient/orderable | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: API returns 400 with a clear field-level error; no order created

Title: API pagination and filtering of radiology orders by status
Preconditions: Multiple orders exist via API
Test_Steps: 1. GET orders?status=active&limit=10 | 2. Request next page | 3. Validate counts
Expected_Result: Results are filtered by status, page size respected, and paging links/cursor return the next set without duplicates

Title: IDOR: cannot access radiology report by guessing/altering identifier
Preconditions: Two patients with reports; authenticated as user without rights to patient B
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain own report URL/ID | 2. Alter the identifier to another patient's report | 3. Request it
Test_Data: Own report id; tampered id for Patient B
Expected_Result: Access is denied; no other patient's report is returned; attempt is logged

Title: Report body sanitizes injected script/markup (stored XSS)
Preconditions: Radiologist can enter free-text findings
Test_Steps: 1. Enter findings containing a script payload | 2. Save and finalize | 3. View report in UI
Expected_Result: Payload is neutralized/escaped on render; no script executes; stored value handled safely

Title: Every report state change writes an immutable audit entry
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; report lifecycle exercised
Test_Steps: 1. Create, preliminary, finalize and addend a report | 2. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Each event records actor, action, timestamp, before/after state; entries are immutable and complete

Title: Image view (PACS access) is logged for PHI access accounting
Preconditions: PACS access logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a study's images | 2. Inspect access/audit log
Expected_Result: An access event records viewing user, patient, accession and timestamp for PHI disclosure accounting

Title: Cancelling an order does not delete its history rows
Preconditions: DB access to verify; order with events
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel an order | 2. Query order/event tables
Test_Data: Order with status events; cancel applied
Expected_Result: Status set to cancelled (logical) with reason; prior event rows retained, not physically deleted; voided/auditable

Title: Referential integrity: report cannot exist without parent order
Preconditions: DB access
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to insert a report row with a non-existent order/accession FK
Expected_Result: Insert is rejected by foreign-key constraint; orphan report is not created

Title: Clinical indication free-text at and beyond max length
Preconditions: Order entry with indication field
Test_Steps: 1. Enter indication at max allowed length | 2. Enter one character beyond max | 3. Save each
Expected_Result: At-max value saves intact; over-max is truncated or rejected per spec with clear message; no overflow error

Title: Scheduling far-future and past datetimes
Preconditions: Scheduling available
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to schedule with a past datetime | 2. Schedule at now+1min | 3. Schedule far future
Expected_Result: Past datetimes are rejected/warned; near-future succeeds; far-future handled without overflow per policy

Title: Pairwise report state x role x action permission matrix
Preconditions: Roles: Radiologist, Resident, Technologist, Clerk; report states
Test_Steps: 1. For each role attempt each action on each report state | 2. Record allow/deny
Expected_Result: Permissions match the RBAC matrix: only credentialed radiologist signs/finalizes; residents draft preliminary; clerks view-only; violations denied

Title: Order routing decision by orderable type and contrast flag
Preconditions: Catalog of orderables with/without contrast
Test_Steps: 1. Place orders across orderable/contrast combinations | 2. Observe safety checks invoked
Expected_Result: Contrast=Yes triggers contrast/renal/allergy checks; XR/US without contrast skip contrast checks; MRI triggers gadolinium-specific path

Title: MRI safety screening (implants/pacemaker/metal) gating
Preconditions: MRI safety screening questionnaire configured
Test_Steps: 1. For each implant status order MRI | 2. Observe gating
Expected_Result: No implant clears; MR-conditional warns with conditions; absolute contraindications block MRI pending safety review

Title: Exploratory: rapid order/cancel/reschedule churn for race conditions
Preconditions: Order present; tester can act quickly
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly schedule, cancel and reschedule the same order | 2. Open in two tabs and act concurrently | 3. Observe state
Expected_Result: Final state is consistent and single-valued; no duplicate accessions, orphaned slots or contradictory statuses; errors handled gracefully

Title: Exploratory: unusual characters and RTL text in findings and patient names
Preconditions: Report entry available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter findings with emoji, RTL, combining and accented characters | 2. Save, finalize and re-open
Test_Data: Text mix: accents, RTL, emoji, long words
Expected_Result: Content is stored and rendered without corruption, truncation or layout break; round-trips intact

Title: Worklist table is keyboard navigable and screen-reader labeled
Preconditions: Worklist UI; assistive tech available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through worklist controls | 2. Sort and filter via keyboard | 3. Inspect ARIA labels/roles on table and status badges
Expected_Result: All interactive elements reachable and operable by keyboard; table headers/status conveyed to screen reader; focus order logical

Title: Contrast-safety alert is announced to assistive technology
Preconditions: Contrast alert can be triggered; screen reader on
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a contrast-allergy alert | 2. Observe screen-reader announcement and focus
Expected_Result: Alert is announced (role=alert/dialog), receives focus, and override controls are labeled and operable without a mouse

Title: Ordering provider notified when critical/STAT report is finalized
Preconditions: Notification channel configured; ordering provider on file
Test_Steps: 1. Finalize a STAT/critical report | 2. Observe notification to ordering provider
Expected_Result: Ordering provider receives a notification with study and patient context; delivery recorded; no PHI over insecure channel

Title: Imaging procedure generates a charge with correct CPT on completion
Preconditions: Charge capture/billing integration enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Complete an imaging study | 2. Inspect generated charge and code
Test_Data: Orderable=CT Abdomen with contrast; expected CPT/charge
Expected_Result: A charge is generated with the correct procedure/CPT code and contrast modifier; linked to encounter and patient

Title: Cancelled (un-performed) study does not generate a charge
Preconditions: Billing integration enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel an order before acquisition | 2. Inspect billing
Expected_Result: No charge is generated for the un-performed study; any prior pending charge is reversed/voided

Title: Duplicate-order detection warns on same study within short window
Preconditions: Duplicate-checking enabled; one matching order placed recently
Test_Steps: 1. Place CT Head order | 2. Within the window place an identical CT Head order
Test_Data: Two identical CT Head orders within 24h
Expected_Result: A duplicate-order warning is shown with prior order details; clinician may confirm intentional duplicate or cancel

Title: Technologist marks exam as exception (patient no-show) with state handling
Preconditions: Scheduled order; technologist role
Test_Steps: 1. Open scheduled order at appointment time | 2. Mark No-Show/Exception | 3. Observe state
Expected_Result: Order moves to an exception/no-show state, freeing the slot; reason captured; eligible for reschedule; auditable

Title: Resident preliminary requires attending co-sign before Final
Preconditions: Resident drafts preliminary; attending co-sign workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Resident saves preliminary report | 2. Attempt to finalize as resident | 3. Attending reviews and co-signs
Expected_Result: Resident cannot finalize alone; attending co-signature required to reach Final; both attributions recorded

Title: Prior contrast reaction history (not formal allergy) surfaces advisory
Preconditions: Patient note/history of prior mild contrast reaction recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Order contrast study for patient with prior-reaction history | 2. Observe advisory
Test_Data: History=mild urticaria with prior contrast; Orderable=CT with contrast
Expected_Result: An advisory surfaces the prior reaction (even without formal allergy record); clinician acknowledges/premedication considered; acknowledgment logged

Title: Worklist reflects order priority change in near real-time
Preconditions: Worklist open; order present as Routine
Test_Steps: 1. Change an order priority from Routine to STAT | 2. Refresh/observe worklist
Expected_Result: Worklist updates to show STAT flag and reorders the item accordingly without requiring re-login

Title: FHIR DiagnosticReport for amended report reflects status amended
Preconditions: Finalized report later amended via addendum; FHIR read scope
Test_Steps: 1. Add an addendum to a final report | 2. GET DiagnosticReport
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport status becomes amended/corrected; presentedForm/conclusion reflects addendum; original retained per versioning
